title: ICU agitation documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports documenting recent hours-to-days changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or an informant.
examination.rationale: Supports entered acute cognitive, perceptual, physical and behavioural changes that may indicate delirium.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting recent hours-to-days changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or an informant.

source.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.1 — risk factors and indicators at presentation

assessment.text: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.
assessment.rationale: Supports documentation and competent CAM-ICU or ICDSC assessment in critical care.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.

source.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.2–1.6.4 — document changes and competent assessment

sources_used.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation

source_limitations: Baseline cognition, onset, physiology, pain, medicines, devices, screening and immediate safety facts are absent.
No delirium diagnosis, cause, sedation, restraint or medicine is generated.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
